title: rubus × quadraticiformis
intro: Rubus × quadraticiformis, also known as the hybrid black raspberry or thimbleberry, is a plant species belonging to the Rosaceae family, resulting from the cross between Rubus argutus and Rubus occidentalis. The medicinal properties of Rubus × quadraticiformis include its high content of anthocyanins, ellagic acid, and other polyphenolic compounds, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with the consumption of Rubus × quadraticiformis include its potential to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease, certain types of cancer, and cognitive decline, as well as its ability to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders. Rubus × quadraticiformis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and jams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
